zh.lifeSummary: Sir Hamon Mascy of Rixton是Robert Massey of Rixton和Rostheme的兒子，於1360年出生在英國爾塔頓。他和Katherine Massey至少有1個女兒。他去世於1360年在英國威爾斯尼德享年0歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Hamon Mascy of Rixton是Robert Massey of Rixton和Rostheme的儿子，于1360年出生在英国爾塔頓。他和Katherine Massey至少有1个女儿。他去世于1360年在英国威尔斯尼德享年0岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mascy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Maxey
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Northamptonshire, so named from the Old English personal name Maccus + Old English ēg ‘island’.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Macasa ‘descendant of Macus’, a personal name of uncertain origin. It may be a variant of Magnus .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Rixton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Angus and Aberdeenshire): habitational name from Roxton Farm in Immingham (Lincolnshire), from the Old English personal name Hrōc (genitive Hrōces) + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Hamon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).

variants.name: Éamann
meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).

meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).

meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).

meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).
